key_highlights: Broad market sell-off with Dow falling over 600 points, Nasdaq declining, and S&P 500's 9-day win streak ending as tech stocks slump.
Geopolitical tensions escalate with US-Iran hostilities, stalling peace talks, and oil price volatility; US Strategic Petroleum Reserve nears 3-year low.
Alphabet raises record $84.75B for AI investments, while Palantir announces key partnerships with Google Cloud and Kirkland & Ellis.
SpaceX IPO at $1.77 trillion valuation and other mega IPOs denied fast index entry by S&P, creating market structure concerns.
Bitcoin crashes triggering billions in liquidations, while Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) sells 32 BTC signaling dividend crisis risk.
Macy's lifts sales outlook and reports strong earnings, defying consumer concerns; Old Dominion shows improving LTL market.
Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) remains at center of AI compute supply chain; Marvell stock climbs toward record high.
Foreign buyers snap up Turkish stocks in fast-track deals; South Korean and Taiwan ETFs see inflows but are concentrated in AI chip stocks.
Private credit deterioration remains slow and contained per Blackstone; Partners Group exposes liquidity mismatch in private equity.
CBS News fires Scott Pelley after clash with new producer; '60 Minutes' undergoes leadership changes.

sectors.trend: Sharp sell-off in AI and chip stocks despite strong fundamentals; Dow hits record on old-economy stocks while Nasdaq declines. Palantir and Marvell show resilience with strategic partnerships and AI adoption. TSM and Broadcom remain key AI beneficiaries but face valuation pressure.
sectors.implications: Buy-the-dip opportunity in high-quality AI names like TSM, Marvell, and Palantir; but caution on overvalued momentum plays. Alphabet's massive AI raise signals long-term commitment but near-term dilution. SOXX ETF hitting 10,000 suggests potential split ahead.

sectors.trend: Oil prices volatile due to US-Iran tensions and ceasefire rumors; US SPR at 3-year low. Aluminum prices hit 4-year high boosting Alcoa. Soybean prices likely bottoming.
sectors.implications: Energy sector remains a hedge against geopolitical risk. Oil supply concerns support prices, but peace talks could cause sharp reversals. Aluminum and soybeans offer tactical commodity plays.

sectors.trend: Macy's lifts outlook and reports strong earnings, defying consumer concerns. AMC stock soared on blockbuster May attendance. All-inclusive travel trend heating up for summer.
sectors.implications: Consumer spending remains resilient, especially in travel and entertainment. Retailers with strong execution (Macy's) are outperforming. Amazon Prime Day (June 23-26) could boost e-commerce.

sectors.trend: Private credit deterioration slow and contained. Partners Group exposes liquidity mismatch in PE. Blackstone-backed Liftoff IPO rises 9.1%. Private equity fundraising faces challenges.
sectors.implications: Private markets face liquidity risks but credit quality remains manageable. IPO market shows signs of life (Liftoff, Innio, Sunshine Silver Mining). Investors should favor liquid alternatives.

sectors.trend: US-Iran hostilities escalate, peace talks stall after Hezbollah rejects truce. Israel won't have say in how Iran war ends. Treasuries rise on oil optimism. Dollar strengthens on strong US economic data.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical risk premium likely to persist. Safe-haven assets (Treasuries, gold) may benefit. Oil supply disruptions could fuel inflation, complicating Fed policy. Jobs report (June 5) will be critical.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin crash triggers billions in liquidations. Strategy (MicroStrategy) sells 32 BTC, signaling dividend crisis risk. Saylor's Bitcoin machine misfiring.
sectors.implications: Crypto market under severe stress. Strategy's BTC sale may indicate liquidity needs or strategic shift. Avoid leveraged crypto plays; consider hedging with put options.

sectors.trend: Old Dominion shows improving LTL market. FedEx Freight IPO could be a 'true winner' per Jim Cramer. Uber stock in focus on robotaxi plans.
sectors.implications: Freight cycle may be bottoming, offering entry points in Old Dominion and FedEx Freight. Autonomous driving (Uber, robotaxis) remains a long-term growth theme.

key_themes: AI investment boom continues but faces near-term profit-taking and valuation concerns
Geopolitical risk (US-Iran) dominates macro outlook, impacting oil, Treasuries, and equities
Market rotation from tech to value/cyclicals as Dow hits record while Nasdaq declines
IPO market revival with SpaceX, Liftoff, Innio, and Sunshine Silver Mining debuts
Consumer resilience despite macro headwinds, supported by travel and entertainment spending
Private equity liquidity mismatch and credit quality concerns
Crypto market crash and Strategy's BTC sale signal risk-off sentiment
Semiconductor supply chain remains critical but faces cyclical and geopolitical risks
US dollar strength on strong economic data, pressuring emerging markets
Corporate AI adoption accelerating via partnerships (Palantir-Google, Palantir-Kirkland & Ellis)

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase allocation to energy, defense, and safe-haven assets (Treasuries, gold). Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and emerging markets sensitive to oil prices.
Diversify into value and cyclical sectors. Consider hedging tech-heavy portfolios with put options or inverse ETFs. Focus on AI names with strong fundamentals (TSM, Marvell, Palantir).

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Oil price spike and inflation resurgence
risk_assessment.mitigation: Add energy exposure (XLE, OIH) and TIPS. Reduce duration in bond portfolios. Monitor Fed reaction function; expect hawkish tilt if oil persists above $90.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Crypto market contagion and Strategy's BTC sale
risk_assessment.mitigation: Avoid leveraged crypto positions. Monitor Strategy's financial health; its BTC sale could signal broader liquidity stress. Consider shorting crypto-exposed equities.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: IPO market froth and liquidity mismatch
risk_assessment.mitigation: Be selective in IPO participation; favor companies with strong fundamentals (SpaceX, Liftoff) over hype. Avoid private credit funds with liquidity mismatches. Favor liquid alternatives.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Fed policy uncertainty ahead of jobs report
risk_assessment.mitigation: Prepare for volatility around June 5 jobs report. JPM's Berro says report won't help Fed. If data strong, expect rate hike fears; if weak, recession fears. Stay nimble with cash reserves.
Diversify across semiconductor value chain (TSM, ASML, Broadcom, Marvell). Monitor geopolitical risks to Taiwan. Consider geographic diversification into US-based fabs.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy the dip in high-quality AI and semiconductor stocks
opportunities.rationale: The tech sell-off is driven by profit-taking and rotation, not fundamentals. TSM, Marvell, and Broadcom remain at the center of AI compute. Alphabet's $85B raise underscores long-term AI commitment. Palantir's partnerships with Google Cloud and Kirkland & Ellis show real enterprise adoption.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase energy exposure as a geopolitical hedge
opportunities.rationale: US-Iran tensions and SPR at 3-year low support oil prices. Alcoa benefits from aluminum price surge. Energy sector offers both upside and defensive characteristics.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in transportation and logistics on cyclical recovery
opportunities.rationale: Old Dominion's improving LTL market and FedEx Freight IPO signal freight cycle bottom. Uber's robotaxi plans offer long-term growth. Consumer resilience supports demand.
SpaceX IPO at $1.77T valuation, Liftoff, and Innio show strong demand. Focus on companies with clear AI/revenue growth stories. Avoid hype-driven IPOs without fundamentals.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Treasuries on dips as safe haven
opportunities.rationale: Geopolitical risk and potential economic slowdown support Treasuries. Oil optimism on peace talks could push yields lower. Use pullbacks to add duration.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce tech exposure and rotate to value/cyclicals
defensive_moves.rationale: Dow hitting record while Nasdaq declines signals rotation. Old-economy stocks (industrials, energy, financials) are outperforming. Reduce overweight in overvalued AI names.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against oil price spike with energy and TIPS
defensive_moves.rationale: US-Iran conflict could disrupt supply further. SPR at 3-year low limits government intervention. TIPS protect against inflation resurgence.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash reserves and reduce leverage
defensive_moves.rationale: Market volatility elevated with geopolitical risks, Fed uncertainty, and crypto crash. Cash provides optionality to buy dips. Avoid margin trading.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid crypto-exposed equities and leveraged plays
defensive_moves.rationale: Bitcoin crash and Strategy's BTC sale signal further downside. Crypto contagion could spread to related stocks. Avoid MicroStrategy, Coinbase, and miners.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Diversify geographically away from concentrated AI chip exposure
defensive_moves.rationale: South Korean and Taiwan ETFs are still just chasing AI chip stocks. Consider broader international diversification (Europe, Japan, EM) to reduce single-country risk.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish with high volatility. Geopolitical risks (US-Iran) and Fed uncertainty dominate. Tech sell-off may continue as rotation to value persists. Oil prices likely to remain elevated, pressuring equities. Jobs report (June 5) will set near-term tone. Crypto crash adds to risk-off sentiment. Support levels for S&P 500 around 5,200; resistance at 5,400.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously bullish. AI investment cycle remains intact with Alphabet, Palantir, and semiconductor leaders driving long-term growth. Freight cycle bottoming supports industrials. IPO market revival (SpaceX, etc.) signals capital market health. However, geopolitical risks and inflation could delay Fed easing. Expect choppy markets with gradual recovery in H2 2026.
key_catalysts: US-Iran peace talks progress or escalation (headline 9, 42, 67)
Fed meeting and rate decision (implied by headline 12, 84)
Oil price trajectory and SPR levels (headline 20, 42, 63)
Crypto market stabilization or further contagion (headline 26, 34, 49)
watch_list: SpaceX IPO demand and aftermarket performance (headline 54, 72, 79)
Macy's and Old Dominion earnings follow-through (headline 2, 15, 48)
Private credit fund redemptions and NAV changes (headline 18, 91)
